$comment: Capsule-Pro generation conventions — the SINGLE source of truth for how Manifest IR is projected into Capsule's API + client. The stock Manifest projections assume flat paths + bare envelopes; Capsule diverges (domain paths, custom envelopes, apiFetch, multi-schema). This file encodes Capsule's conventions so generated routes AND the generated client agree with each other and with hand-written code. Consumed by manifest/scripts/generate-capsule-client.mjs (and, going forward, generate.mjs). See manifest/contract-alignment-plan.md.

routePaths.$comment: entity -> route base. Reads live under the DOMAIN base; writes go through the canonical dispatcher. Source of truth for the base is manifest/scripts/entity-domain-map.mjs (ENTITY_DOMAIN_MAP); templates below build full paths from it.

commandOverrides.$comment: entity.command -> explicit legacy/domain route path, used only where a route enriches the request (e.g. signature ipAddress) and hasn't moved to the dispatcher yet.

readEnvelope.$comment: Shape of generated GET responses (via manifestSuccessResponse). The list payload key is the camelCase PLURAL entity name; detail is the camelCase singular. Always fall back to `data` for hand-written/legacy routes.

commandEnvelope.$comment: Shape of ALL command (write) responses. Uniform across the dispatcher (run-manifest-command-core) and the legacy handler (manifest-command-handler).

schemaPlacement.$comment: Prisma @@schema placement — multi-schema is NOT native to the projection (per official docs), so Capsule applies it via the placement policy post-process.

dateSerialization.$comment: API sends ISO 8601 strings or null. UI formats with the single safe helper; raw Intl.DateTimeFormat.format(value) on a non-Date is banned (throws 'Invalid time value').
dateSerialization.ban: Intl.DateTimeFormat().format(value) where value is not a Date
